posts.text:
The US manufacturing recovery continues:

ISM Manufacturing PMI fell -0.2 points in February, to 52.4, marking the 2nd consecutive monthly expansion.

New orders fell -1.3 points, to 55.8, the 2nd-highest since February 2022.

The employment index rose +0.7 points, to 48.8, but remained in contraction for its 13th consecutive month.

Meanwhile, prices paid spiked +11.5 points, to 70.5, the highest since June 2022, raising fears of an inflation resurgence.

This surge in input costs came before the new Middle East tensions, which significantly limited oil tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and pushed crude oil prices sharply higher.

If energy prices remain elevated, manufacturers may have little choice but to pass costs on to retailers and consumers.

The manufacturing recovery is alive, but the inflation threat seems to be back.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text:
Here are 10 things fractal graphene can do that ideal 2D graphene can’t:
1.Disperse uniformly at scale — Its 3D fractal morphology prevents the aggregation that makes flat graphene clump and lose performance in real-world matrices.
2.Reinforce composites at ultra-low loadings — Delivers meaningful mechanical gains at 0.01–0.05 wt%, where flat graphene requires 10–100x more material to achieve comparable results.
3.Strengthen concrete — At just 0.02 wt%, it increases compressive strength by 21% and allows 16.7% cement reduction. Flat graphene cannot disperse well enough in cementitious systems to achieve this.
4.Maintain accessible surface area in practice — Fractal porosity keeps surface area usable. Flat graphene restacks in solution, burying most of its theoretical ~2,630 m²/g surface area.
5.Provide 3D structural stability — Turbostratic stacking adds out-of-plane rigidity while retaining in-plane flexibility, a combination single-layer graphene structurally cannot offer.
6.Resist environmental doping — Decoupled turbostratic layers are far less sensitive to substrate effects and atmospheric contaminants that destabilize pristine 2D graphene devices.
7.Enable electrothermal actuation — Its porous, high-edge-density network supports silent, high-force electrothermal actuators — a function not achievable with flat graphene films.
http://8.Support fractal magnetic states — The unique geometry enables emergent magnetic phenomena not observed in ideal flat graphene lattices.
9.Integrate into 3D-printing and conductive inks — Its fractal geometry and reactive derivatives allow direct incorporation into additive manufacturing and printed electronics at scale.
10.Deliver batch-to-batch consistency at commercial volumes — Flat graphene production methods yield variable quality. HydroGraph’s detonation synthesis produces 99.8% purity fractal graphene with reproducible performance — a prerequisite for industrial adoption that ideal 2D graphene production cannot yet match.
posts.author: BambroughKevin
